question.text: Which document serves as the primary and most comprehensive source of information about a mutual fund scheme for investors?

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** The Scheme Information Document (SID) is the primary document that contains all scheme-specific information including investment objectives, asset allocation, risk factors, fees, fund manager details, and performance data. It is the most comprehensive mandatory document about a specific scheme.
**The Trap:** SAI contains statutory information common to all schemes of an AMC (sponsor, trustee, custodian details) — not scheme-specific details. KIM is a summarized version for quick reference. Fact Sheets are non-mandatory periodic documents.
**Cross-Exam Context:** In NISM V-B and XII exams, understanding the hierarchy of scheme documents (SID > SAI > KIM) is critical for compliance questions.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When a client asks detailed questions about a scheme's investment strategy, asset allocation, or fee structure, always refer them to the SID first. It is the single most important document a distributor should thoroughly understand before recommending any scheme.
